# Encompass API ‚Äî Authoritative reference (owner-supplied 2026-07-22)

Snapshot of the Encompass Developer Connect docs the owner pasted into chat
during the Phase-2 bulk-pull debug on 2026-07-22. Kept here so every future
Claude session can consult it without re-fetching (my sandbox can't reach
`developer.icemortgagetechnology.com` ‚Äî 403 at the egress proxy).

**Do NOT edit this file to reflect wishful thinking; only update it when the
owner pastes a newer snapshot from ICE.** Everything below is quoted from the
ICE Developer Connect docs. Source URL prefixes: `https://developer.icemortgagetechnology.com/developer-connect/`.

## V1 vs V3 ‚Äî which loan APIs to call

- **V3 is the modern REST base**; V1 is a RESTful wrapper on legacy SOAP that
  ICE says will eventually deprecate. **For new work, always prefer V3 when it
  exists; fall back to V1 only when V3 doesn't cover a case.** Both are stable.
- V3 loan schema uses standard loan JSON with JSON paths; V3 fixed-size
  collections have no `id` (identified by metadata), variable-size collections
  carry a system-generated unique id.
- PILOT's current usage: `/encompass/v3/loans/{guid}` (get loan),
  `/encompass/v3/loanPipeline` (search), `/encompass/v3/settings/loan/*`
  (field-catalog metadata). All V3. ‚úÖ

## OAuth token endpoint

`POST https://api.elliemae.com/oauth2/v1/token`

Content-Type `application/x-www-form-urlencoded`. The two grants PILOT uses:

### Resource Owner Password Credentials (ROPC ‚Äî most tenants)

```
grant_type = password
username   = <encompass_user>@encompass:<instance_id>
password   = <user_password>
client_id  = <api_client_id>
client_secret = <api_client_secret>
```

Verified working against instance BE11397907 on 2026-07-22.
`ENCOMPASS_CLIENT_ID` + `_SECRET` alone (client_credentials fallback) returned
`400 unsupported_grant_type` on this tenant ‚Äî ROPC required.

### Client Credentials (ISV partners only)

```
grant_type    = client_credentials
instance_id   = <instance_id>
scope         = lp
Authorization = Basic <base64(client_id:client_secret)>
```

Per the ICE docs: **"grant type client_credentials is for ISV partners only.
Lenders must use grant type = password."** PILOT is a lender integration ‚Äî use ROPC.

### Access token behavior

- Active 30 min, up to 24h if refreshed.
- Must be used within 15 min or it expires early.
- Bearer header: `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>`.

## Pipeline search ‚Äî the second read-shaped POST

`POST https://api.elliemae.com/encompass/v3/loanPipeline`

**This is a QUERY endpoint (read-only in intent) ‚Äî the body carries the search
filter because it doesn't fit in URL query params.** PILOT's `_fetchGuarded`
guard allows this POST as one of exactly two allowlisted read-shaped endpoints
(alongside the OAuth token exchange).

### Correct request body shape (learned the hard way, 2026-07-22)

```json
{
  "filter": {
    "operator": "and",
    "terms": [
      { "canonicalName": "Loan.LoanNumber", "value": "YS-123", "matchType": "exact" }
    ]
  },
  "sortOrder": [
    { "canonicalName": "Loan.LastModified", "order": "desc" }
  ],
  "fields": [
    "Loan.Guid", "Loan.LoanNumber", "Loan.LoanAmount", "Loan.LastModified"
  ]
}
```

**`sortOrder` MUST be at the TOP LEVEL** of the body ‚Äî nesting it inside
`filter` returns HTTP 400 with `queryContract.filter.sortOrder Invalid field
name or value`. Empty `filter` should be OMITTED entirely (Encompass returns
all loans visible to the token). `fields` is optional ‚Äî omitting returns just
the loan GUIDs.

### Pagination

Query params: `?limit=N&start=M` (offset-based). PILOT's bulk pull uses
`limit=200&start=0`, then advances `start` by `page.length` until a short
page. **Do NOT try to paginate via a `Loan.LastModified` filter term** ‚Äî the
canonical name isn't supported as a filter, and even if it were, ties on
LastModified would skip/duplicate rows.

## Loan resource ‚Äî GET

`GET /encompass/v3/loans/{loanGuid}`

Returns the full raw loan JSON. Optional query params: `entities=<comma-list>`
to limit sub-entities, `view=<mode>` for view mode. Returns a nested object
with top-level `guid`, `loanNumber`, `applications[]` (each with `borrower`,
`coBorrower`), `customFields[]`, plus many other domain sections.

Custom fields ride in the top-level `customFields[]` array as
`{fieldName, stringValue, numericValue, dateValue, ...}` ‚Äî the concrete
`*Value` key depends on the tenant's declared type.

## Field catalog endpoints (all GET; PILOT pulls nightly into
`encompass_field_catalog`)

- `GET /encompass/v3/settings/loan/customFields` ‚Äî tenant's custom fields
  (e.g. `CUST01FV` = "DSCR" on BE11397907; ~855 slots total, most unused).
- `GET /encompass/v3/settings/loan/standardFields` ‚Äî the canonical fields.
- `GET /encompass/v3/settings/loan/enums` ‚Äî picklist values for enum fields.
- `GET /encompass/v3/settings/loan/milestones` ‚Äî the tenant's milestone list.
- `GET /encompass/v3/settings/loan/folders` ‚Äî loan folder names.
- `GET /encompass/v3/settings/loan/loanTemplates` ‚Äî loan template paths.

Also `GET /encompass/v1/schema/loan` (whole loan schema) if we ever need the
JSON schema itself instead of the catalog. PILOT doesn't need this today.

## HTTP status codes

- **200 / 201 / 202 / 204** ‚Äî success.
- **400** ‚Äî bad request (payload shape wrong ‚Äî see the pipeline example above).
- **401** ‚Äî unauthorized (bad or expired token).
- **403** ‚Äî forbidden (role/persona lacks access).
- **404** ‚Äî resource not found.
- **409** ‚Äî conflict.
- **429** ‚Äî rate-limit exceeded.
- **500 / 503** ‚Äî server error / maintenance.

## Loan Data Dictionary ‚Äî how PILOT should build its own catalog

Per ICE's "Encompass Loan Data Dictionary Guide," the recommended baseline is:

1. `GET /encompass/v3/schema/loan` ‚Äî the full loan JSON schema. Refreshed on
   every Encompass release.
2. `GET /encompass/v3/schema/field` ‚Äî standard field definitions.
3. `GET /encompass/v3/settings/loan/customFields` ‚Äî tenant custom fields.

PILOT's `reader.refreshFieldCatalog()` already covers `settings/loan/*`; adding
the `schema/loan` + `schema/field` pulls is a Phase-3 enhancement if we need
JSON-schema-driven validation.

## Webhooks (Phase 3+ opportunity ‚Äî not built yet)

Encompass supports webhook subscriptions on the loan resource (via `POST
/webhook/v1/subscriptions`). Events include `change`, `milestone`, `create`,
`delete`, plus the premium Enhanced Field Change (EFC) event. Each notification
carries an `eventId`, `resourceRef`, and `payload` including changed fields.

- Signing key delivered on subscription ‚Äî every notification carries
  `X-Elli-Signature` (HMAC of the body); PILOT MUST verify before processing.
- Failed delivery retried up to 4 times, then discarded ‚Äî pair with the
  Webhook Event History API for reconciliation.
- Timeout: 30s per delivery attempt; server error, timeout, or connection
  error = failed attempt.

**PILOT is currently POLL-based (fine for Phase 2). Webhooks would replace the
15-min poll with near-realtime.** Requires: a public HTTPS callback URL on
PILOT (`/api/inbound/encompass/webhook`), signature verification, and event
deduplication by `eventId`.

## What this means for the READ-ONLY freeze

Everything above is READ traffic. The webhook subscription POST is a
configuration change on Encompass ‚Äî technically a WRITE to the webhook resource
(not the loan) ‚Äî so wiring webhooks would require a **separate, explicit
owner-approved exception** to the freeze (adding `POST /webhook/v1/subscriptions`
to the `POST_ALLOWLIST` in `src/lib/integrations/encompass.js`, with the same
"read-shaped / no mutation of loan data" justification we used for
`loanPipeline`). Not doing that unless the owner says so in their own words.
